behavior_summary: Responds to the item's 'U8P_TREE::look' (examine) event by composing a short descriptive message beginning with the literal "tree ".
Delivers that message to the inspecting process/player by calling Item::bark with the constructed string and the viewer's process id.
Does not modify persistent game state or global flags; its effect is purely communicative/visual to the viewer.
Allocates/fills a temporary string buffer for the message, passes it to Item::bark, frees the temporary, and then processes the return value before suspending/returning.
notable_control_flow: Entry on U8P_TREE::look/examine event -> build message string (prefix "tree ") -> push viewer pid and string -> call Item::bark -> free temporary string -> handle return value (implies/pop) -> suspend/return.
No loops, no branching that changes game state; control flow is linear and event-driven.
open_questions: What additional item-specific text (if any) is concatenated to the literal "tree " inside the constructed_string? The disassembly shows allocation and concatenation but not the exact source of any suffix.
Is BP+06h always the viewer/process id in every caller context, or are there edge cases where a different pid is passed? (Inference from stack usage is high but not formally verified).
Are there any localized side-effects of Item::bark (e.g., logging, UI focus changes) not visible in this script that might affect player experience?

main_function.name: U8P_TREE::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the object's U8P_TREE::look/examine event by constructing a short description starting with "tree " and asking the item to 'bark' that message to the inspecting process/player.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — the U8P_TREE::look/examine event triggered when an actor inspects or looks at the tree item.

function_map.name: U8P_TREE::look
function_map.role: U8P_TREE::look/examine event handler for the tree item

external_calls.purpose: Deliver/display the constructed text message ("tree ...") to the viewer/process id provided; act as the item's output mechanism for descriptions.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Responds to the item's 'U8P_TREE::look' (examine) event by composing a short descriptive message beginning with the literal "tree ".
